ref: Jacob set up a tombstone over her grave, and it is still there.
text: Ní Jaakɔb dū ditaŋkpal ki cóoǹ dikaakul pu án nīn sá Raseel kaakul daaŋku. Di bí ki dāa sāā díǹ.
translated: And Jacob went on his journey and came to the land of the eastern people. And he saw a well in the field, and there were three flocks of sheep lying there by it; for they watered the flocks from that well, and the stone on the well's mouth was large.

ref: long years to earn your two daughters and another
text: Ní Jaakɔb bál̀ Laaban uaranja ki yíkì kaa kāaǹ-u.
translated: And Jacob deceived Laban the Aramean, by not telling him.

ref: and Potiphar's wife soon noticed him. She asked him to make love to her,
text: An dāa bāań ŋyunti ŋubɔee ní u-kpiliu nimpuu dū u-nimbiliŋ ki mántì u-pu ní ki bí-u yii: «Dāan̄ kí dòon̄ nì mi.»
translated: The man who was sitting there heard a person shouting and he turned around to see who it was.

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Ní Unimbɔti nìkī ki bí Abraam yii: «Siiyee, á nīn ŋúb́ m-poondi, kí nīn ŋáań min m lèe, sii nì a-naantiibi bin dáá bíń boon ki dòońee kɔkɔ.
translated: The Lord said to Abram: "Go out from your country, your relatives, and your father's house to the land that I will show you.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
